title: Critical mechanical structures: topology and entropy
speaker: Xiaoming Mao
abstract: Critical mechanical structures are structures at the verge of mechanical instability. These structures are characterized by their floppy modes, which are deformations costing little energy. On the one hand, numerous interesting phenomena in soft matter are governed by the physics of critical mechanical structures, because they capture the critical state between solid and liquid.
tags: Workshop on Topology: Identifying Order in Complex Systems

proof_stages.label: Floppy modes as mechanisms near instability
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines floppy modes as zero- or low-energy deformations and motivates them as the degrees of freedom controlling mechanical instability and mechanism design.

proof_stages.label: Maxwell-Calladine counting and isostaticity
proof_stages.summary: The speaker derives the count of zero modes from degrees of freedom, constraints, and states of self-stress, then obtains the large-system isostatic criterion z=2d for central-force networks.

proof_stages.label: Periodic Maxwell lattices and the location of zero modes
proof_stages.summary: Restricting to periodic lattices allows exact phonon calculations and shows that Maxwell lattices can have bulk zero-mode lines or boundary-localized modes depending on geometry and self-stress.

proof_stages.label: Kane-Lubensky topological polarization
proof_stages.summary: The Kane-Lubensky index theorem is introduced to explain how a topological polarization, computed from the winding of the equilibrium matrix, redistributes floppy modes among edges.

proof_stages.label: Guest-mode path through a topological transition
proof_stages.summary: Uniform soft twistings provide a global deformation coordinate along which a mechanical lattice can change topological polarization, causing edge modes to pass through a bulk-floppy critical state and switching boundary stiffness by orders of magnitude.

proof_stages.label: Elasticity classification from uniform soft twistings
proof_stages.summary: Starting from a uniform soft strain and imposing compatibility of deformations, the speaker classifies critical structures into dilation-dominant systems with surface waves on all edges and shear-dominant systems that can support bulk waves and topological polarization.

proof_stages.label: Entropic effects of many floppy modes
proof_stages.summary: Thermal fluctuations over many floppy modes produce order-by-disorder, shift stability boundaries, and in topological lattices may favor or sharply cross the critical angles where bulk floppy modes appear.
